feature.name: Interactive Sandbox Hero — Personalized App Demo

summary: Build a pixel-perfect interactive sandbox of the full 60 app as the website hero. Two entry points: homepage (URL input → research → sandbox) and campaign links (/t/{code} → instant personalized sandbox). Maximum personalization — visitor's company as a deal, their contacts as stakeholders, AI-drafted email to them. Full app chrome with clickable navigation.

phases.name: Sandbox Shell
phases.description: Build the app chrome replica — sidebar, topbar, internal router, data provider

phases.name: Core Views
phases.description: Build the 5 key interactive views: Pipeline, Dashboard, Meeting Prep, Email Draft, Contacts

phases.name: Personalization Engine
phases.description: Transform research data into full app dataset with AI-generated content

phases.name: Homepage Integration
phases.description: Wire sandbox into homepage flow: URL input → research → sandbox transition

phases.name: /t/{code} Campaign System
phases.description: Database, routes, pre-enrichment pipeline, and instant sandbox loading for campaign links

phases.name: Interactive Copilot
phases.description: Command center / Quick Add demo with simulated AI responses

phases.name: Tracking & Alerts
phases.description: Engagement scoring, Slack webhook alerts, and lead pipeline integration

phases.name: Polish & Performance
phases.description: Lazy loading, animation optimization, mobile responsiveness, guided tour

stories.title: Create SandboxDataProvider context and mock data types
acceptance: SandboxDataProvider React context created at packages/landing/src/sandbox/data/SandboxDataProvider.tsx
TypeScript interfaces for SandboxDeal, SandboxContact, SandboxMeeting, SandboxActivity, SandboxKPI
Context provides: deals[], contacts[], meetings[], activities[], kpis, currentUser, currentOrg
Default mock dataset with 5 deals, 12 contacts, 4 meetings, 20 activities, and KPI metrics
Context consumed via useSandboxData() hook

stories.title: Build SandboxSidebar — faithful replica of app sidebar navigation
acceptance: SandboxSidebar.tsx renders at ~240px width with collapse toggle
Navigation items match real app: Dashboard, Pipeline, Contacts, Companies, Meetings, Calls, Email
Each nav item has Lucide icon, label, and active state with violet accent
Clicking nav items updates sandbox internal view (via callback prop)
Matches app design: bg-gray-950 background, border-gray-800 right border, Inter font
Bottom section shows mock user avatar + org name

stories.title: Build SandboxTopbar — logo, notifications, and user menu
acceptance: SandboxTopbar.tsx renders 64px fixed height bar
Left: 60 logo (text) matching app branding
Right: notification bell (with dot indicator), search icon, user avatar dropdown
Notification bell shows mock count badge (3)
Matches app design: bg-gray-950, border-b border-gray-800, backdrop-blur-md
User dropdown shows demo user name + 'Demo Mode' badge

stories.title: Build SandboxApp shell — compose sidebar + topbar + content area with view router
acceptance: SandboxApp.tsx composes SandboxTopbar + SandboxSidebar + main content area
Internal view state managed by useState (not React Router)
Views: 'dashboard' | 'pipeline' | 'contacts' | 'meetings' | 'email' | 'copilot'
Sidebar nav clicks switch the active view with Framer Motion crossfade transition
Main content area has proper layout: pl-[240px] pt-16 (sidebar + topbar offset)
Wrapped in SandboxDataProvider
Renders at fixed aspect ratio (16:10) with browser-chrome frame on desktop, full-width on mobile

stories.title: Build SandboxPipeline — Kanban board with deal cards
acceptance: SandboxPipeline.tsx renders a horizontal Kanban with 4-5 stage columns
Columns: Lead, Qualified, Proposal, Negotiation, Closed Won
Each column has 1-3 SandboxDealCard components
Deal cards show: company avatar (gradient fallback), company name, deal value, owner initials, health indicator
Visitor's company highlighted with violet border glow + 'Your company' badge
Cards are visually draggable (cursor: grab) but don't actually reorder
Matches app Pipeline design: bg-gray-900 cards, rounded-xl, health bar colors

stories.title: Build SandboxDashboard — KPI cards, activity feed, and sales chart
acceptance: SandboxDashboard.tsx renders a metrics overview page
Top row: 4 KPI cards (Meetings This Week, Deals in Pipeline, Revenue Pipeline, Follow-ups Pending)
Each KPI shows value, trend arrow (up/down), and percentage change
Activity feed section: 5-8 recent activities (calls, emails, meetings) with timestamps
Optional: simple bar/line chart for weekly activity (CSS-only or lightweight SVG, no Recharts)
Matches app Dashboard design system

stories.title: Build SandboxMeetingPrep — AI-generated meeting preparation doc
acceptance: SandboxMeetingPrep.tsx renders a meeting prep view for a hypothetical call with the visitor's company
Header: meeting title, date/time, attendee avatars
Sections: Company Overview, Talking Points (3-5), Risk Signals, Questions to Ask, Deal Context
Each section has an icon header and content with bullet points
Content is personalized from SandboxDataProvider (company name, industry, contacts)
Matches app meeting intelligence design patterns

stories.title: Build SandboxEmailDraft — AI-generated follow-up email composer
acceptance: SandboxEmailDraft.tsx renders an email composition view
To: field pre-filled with visitor's name/email (from sandbox data)
Subject: contextual follow-up subject line
Body: AI-style drafted email with greeting, body paragraphs, sign-off
Email content references the visitor's company, recent meeting context, and next steps
Action buttons: Send, Edit, Regenerate (visual only, no real action)
Side panel: 'Why this email' showing AI reasoning context

stories.title: Build SandboxContacts — contact list with relationship signals
acceptance: SandboxContacts.tsx renders a contact list/table view
Columns: Avatar, Name, Title, Company, Engagement Level, Last Interaction
8-12 contacts displayed, visitor's company contacts grouped at top
Engagement level shown as colored badges (Hot, Warm, Cold)
Click a contact row to show a detail side panel with contact info + interaction timeline
Matches app contact list design patterns

stories.title: Build generatePersonalizedData — transform ResearchData into full sandbox dataset
acceptance: generatePersonalizedData() function in packages/landing/src/sandbox/data/generatePersonalizedData.ts
Takes ResearchData (from useDemoResearch) + optional visitor info (name, email)
Returns complete SandboxData object: deals, contacts, meetings, activities, kpis
Visitor's company is always Deal #1 in 'Proposal' stage with $50-200K value (based on company size)
Generates 3-4 additional realistic deals as pipeline context
Creates 3-5 contacts from the visitor's company (from research data contacts/team)
Meeting prep content derived from research company overview + competitive landscape
Email draft personalized with visitor's name and company context

stories.title: Build AI content generation for personalized email and meeting prep
acceptance: Edge function sandbox-personalize at supabase/functions/sandbox-personalize/index.ts
POST endpoint accepting: company_name, domain, visitor_name, visitor_title, research_summary
Returns: { email_draft, meeting_prep, talking_points }
Uses Gemini Flash for fast generation (< 3s response time)
Email draft: warm, professional follow-up referencing their company's specifics
Meeting prep: 5 talking points, 3 risk signals, 3 questions to ask
Proper CORS headers via getCorsHeaders(req), no JWT required (public endpoint)

stories.title: Build animated sandbox entrance — research-to-app morph transition
acceptance: SandboxEntrance.tsx handles the transition from research phase to sandbox
Animation: Agent research cards morph/collapse into the sidebar, topbar assembles from top
Content area fades in with staggered children (dashboard view by default)
Total transition duration: 1.5-2s with spring physics
Framer Motion AnimatePresence for smooth mount/unmount
Falls back to simple fade-in if prefers-reduced-motion is set

stories.title: Wire sandbox into homepage flow — URL input → research → sandbox
acceptance: LandingPage.tsx updated: after research completes, transitions to SandboxApp instead of ProductShowcase
Flow: Hero URL input → AgentResearch animation → SandboxEntrance → SandboxApp
generatePersonalizedData() called with research results to populate sandbox
sandbox-personalize edge function called in parallel for email/meeting content
Back button or 'Exit demo' returns to homepage hero
Sandbox renders in a contained section (not full-page takeover) with browser-chrome frame
Existing demo-v2 flow preserved as fallback route at /demo-v2

stories.title: Add guided tour overlay with feature callouts
acceptance: SandboxTour.tsx provides a step-by-step guided highlight overlay
5 tour stops: Dashboard → Pipeline (their company deal) → Meeting Prep → Email Draft → Copilot
Each stop: spotlight (dim surrounding), tooltip with description, Next/Skip buttons
Tour auto-advances after 5s idle or on click
Tour state persisted so returning visitors don't re-see it
Dismissable at any point, sandbox remains fully interactive after

stories.title: Create campaign_links table migration and RLS policies
acceptance: Migration creates campaign_links table with: code (PK), email, domain, first_name, last_name, company_name, enrichment (JSONB), campaign_id, org_id (FK), created_at, expires_at, first_visit, visit_count, engagement (JSONB), score
Index on code column for fast lookups
Index on org_id + campaign_id for listing
RLS: org members can read/write their own campaign links
RLS: anonymous users can read by code (for /t/{code} resolution)
TTL default: 30 days from creation

stories.title: Build /t/:code route and campaign link resolver
acceptance: New route /t/:code added to packages/landing/src/App.tsx
CampaignLanding.tsx page component resolves code via Supabase query
On valid code: loads enrichment data, increments visit_count, sets first_visit if null
Passes enrichment data to generatePersonalizedData() → SandboxApp
Welcome header: 'Hey {first_name}, here's what 60 would do for {company_name}'
On invalid/expired code: redirect to homepage with toast message
Page view tracked with campaign_id as utm_campaign

stories.title: Build pre-enrichment edge function for batch campaign link generation
acceptance: Edge function campaign-enrich at supabase/functions/campaign-enrich/index.ts
POST endpoint accepting: contacts[] (email, first_name, last_name, company_name), campaign_id, org_id
For each contact: extract domain → call landing-research (Gemini + Exa) → generate 6-char code
Code generation: crypto.randomBytes(6) base62 encoded, collision-checked
Store results in campaign_links table with enrichment JSONB
Rate limited: max 10 concurrent enrichments
Returns: Array of { email, code, link } for campaign integration
JWT-protected (org members only)

stories.title: Build campaign management UI — create links, upload contacts, view analytics
acceptance: CampaignManager component accessible from main app settings or tools
Create campaign: name, upload CSV (email, first_name, last_name, company_name)
Trigger batch enrichment via campaign-enrich edge function
Progress display: X/Y contacts enriched with status indicators
Results table: contact name, company, generated link (copy button), enrichment status
Export links as CSV for email campaign tools (Instantly, etc.)
Basic analytics: visit count, avg engagement score per campaign

stories.title: Build SandboxCopilot — command center with simulated AI interaction
acceptance: SandboxCopilot.tsx renders a command center overlay (triggered by sidebar Copilot nav or Cmd+K)
Chat-style interface with message bubbles
Pre-loaded conversation: user asks 'Prepare for my meeting with {company}' → AI responds with prep
Typing animation for AI responses (30-50ms per character)
Response includes: meeting context, talking points, risk signals (from sandbox data)
Input field allows typing (visual only) with suggested prompts below

stories.title: Build SandboxQuickAdd — modal with AI cascade animation
acceptance: SandboxQuickAdd.tsx renders a Quick Add modal matching the app's pattern
Auto-plays a demo sequence: user types command → AI processes in steps
4-step cascade: Search meetings → Pull deal context → Research contacts → Generate prep doc
Each step: loading spinner → green checkmark, staggered 600ms
Spring animation for modal entry (y: 100% → 0, damping: 30, stiffness: 300)
Result card appears below with meeting prep summary
Triggerable from SandboxTopbar search icon or keyboard shortcut indicator

stories.title: Build sandbox interaction tracking and engagement scoring
acceptance: useSandboxTracking() hook tracks all sandbox interactions
Events tracked: view_switch, deal_card_click, contact_click, email_view, copilot_open, time_per_view
Each event has a weight (from website_brief.md scoring table)
Running engagement score computed and stored in localStorage
For /t/{code} visitors: engagement JSONB and score updated on campaign_links row
Edge function sandbox-track for fire-and-forget event storage

stories.title: Build Slack webhook alerts for hot sandbox leads
acceptance: Edge function sandbox-alert at supabase/functions/sandbox-alert/index.ts
Triggered when engagement score crosses 'Hot' threshold (51+)
Slack message format: visitor name, company, score, time on site, top interests, demo completion %
Includes action links: View in 60, Send follow-up
Debounced: max 1 alert per visitor per 24h
Configurable Slack webhook URL via org settings or env var

stories.title: Build lead pipeline integration — sandbox visitors become leads in 60
acceptance: When a sandbox visitor signs up, their enrichment data seeds into their 60 account
Company from research becomes a real company record
Contacts from enrichment become real contact records
Demo deal becomes a real deal in their pipeline
Meeting prep and email draft preserved as starting content
Signup form pre-fills email (from /t/{code} data or partial signup tracking)

stories.title: Performance optimization — lazy loading, code splitting, and animation budgets
acceptance: Sandbox components lazy-loaded via React.lazy() — not in initial bundle
Vite manual chunk: 'sandbox' chunk separated from main landing page code
Sandbox chunk budget: < 150KB gzipped total
LCP stays < 1.5s (hero renders before sandbox loads)
Framer Motion animations use transform + opacity only (GPU-accelerated)
prefers-reduced-motion: all animations instant, no spring physics
Below-fold sections intersection-observer lazy-initialized

stories.title: Mobile-responsive sandbox and touch gesture support
acceptance: Sandbox renders correctly on mobile (< 768px): sidebar collapses to bottom tab bar
Topbar simplified on mobile: logo + hamburger menu
Pipeline view: horizontal scroll with snap points on mobile
Dashboard: single-column KPI cards stack
Touch gestures: swipe between views, pull-down to return to hero
All touch targets minimum 44px
Simplified animations on mobile (fewer staggers, shorter durations)

parallelGroups.reason: Sidebar and Topbar are independent components
All 5 views only depend on SBX-001 (data provider), no overlap
Data transform, edge function, and entrance animation are independent
Route resolver and enrichment function both depend on schema but not each other
Performance and mobile optimization are independent concerns
